subject: Project Management
title: CERN's Large Hadron Collider
description: Learn CERN's Large Hadron Collider in one minute.

hook.text: Under the Swiss-French border sits a 27 km ring that smashes particles at nearly the speed of light.

buildup.text: The LHC took 10 years, 10,000 scientists from 100 countries, and $4.75 billion to build.

discovery.text: Superconducting magnets cooled to -271°C bend particles around the ring 11,000 times per second.

twist.text: Days after launch in 2008, a faulty solder joint caused an explosion. Repairs took 14 months.

climax.text: In 2012, the LHC discovered the Higgs boson — confirming how particles get mass.

punchline.text: The biggest machine ever built found the smallest thing.

quiz.question: What did the Large Hadron Collider discover in 2012?
